name: Mocha Mousse2
recipeIngredient: 6 ounces high quality milk chocolate (e.g. Lindt)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the milk chocolate into small pieces.
2. Place the chocolate, coffee, and rum in a medium heatproof bowl.
3. Bring 1 inch of water to a simmer in a wide skillet.
4. Turn off the heat and let the mixture cool for 30 seconds (milk chocolate burns easily—do not turn the heat back on unless necessary).
5. Set the bowl of chocolate in the pan of hot water.
6. Stir the chocolate mixture constantly until melted and smooth.
7. Remove the chocolate from the heat and allow it to cool until a small amount feels slightly cool, about 85°F.
8. Whip the egg whites until stiff but not dry, and fold half into the chocolate.
9. In a separate bowl, whip the cream until just before soft peaks form.
10. Add 1/4 cup powdered sugar and gently fold half of the cream into the cooled chocolate mixture (always stir in the same direction to avoid breakage).
11. To the remaining half of the cream, add Grand Marnier and grated orange peel; then fold the remaining egg whites into this mixture.
12. Layer the chocolate and orange mousse mixtures in a wine glass.
13. Chill for at least two hours before serving.
14. Decorate with fresh strawberries.
